file_insights.purpose: Network connectivity monitoring service using reactive Flow pattern
file_insights.complexity: Medium - handles async callbacks and Flow lifecycle

file_insights.issue: Build artifacts should not be committed - should be in .gitignore

patterns_discovered.description: Uses Kotlin Flow with callbackFlow to expose network state as reactive observable
Automatic registration/unregistration of callbacks based on Flow collection lifecycle
patterns_discovered.implementation: awaitClose block for cleanup on cancellation

patterns_discovered.description: Sends current network state before registering for future changes
Verifies NET_CAPABILITY_INTERNET instead of just connection existence
Uses distinctUntilChanged() operator to avoid redundant state emissions

gotchas_discovered.description: .gradle/ directory files should typically be excluded from version control
ConnectivityManager.registerNetworkCallback requires Android API 21+, not explicitly documented
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add @RequiresApi annotation and API level documentation

gotchas_discovered.description: Small race condition between initial state check and callback registration, though mitigated by order
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Current implementation handles this correctly by sending initial state first

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No error handling in callback methods
gotchas_discovered.description: Network callbacks don't handle potential exceptions in trySend operations
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add try-catch or error handling logic

approach_outcome.achievement_summary: Successfully implemented NetworkMonitor with reactive Flow-based network connectivity observation
key_accomplishments: Proper resource cleanup and lifecycle management
approach_outcome.completeness: Complete - feature fully implements connectivity observation as designed

recommendations.recommendation: Add .gradle/ directory to .gitignore to prevent build artifacts from being tracked
Add @RequiresApi(21) annotation to indicate minimum Android version requirement
Consider receiving ConnectivityManager through dependency injection instead of context parameter
Add error handling in callback methods to gracefully handle trySend() failures
Add public method to force current state check for unit testing purposes
Consider adding permission documentation for INTERNET and ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE requirements
